what_it_does: Implements model inventory and model swap for local inference services: inventory() lists HuggingFace Hub cached models and Ollama models; swap() updates a registry service's active model either by setting config.model or by rewriting the MODEL default in its serve script, then restarts the systemd user unit.
observations: inventory() scans ~/.cache/huggingface/hub and runs du -sh for each models--* directory, then runs ollama list and formats the output.
swap() supports two service flavors: config-driven (sets svc['config']['model']) and legacy script-based (regex rewrite of MODEL=\"${1:-...}\").
The legacy branch checks svc['manage']['type'] != 'user-unit' twice, with the second check redundant.
No backup or atomic write is performed when rewriting the serve script.
model_id is interpolated directly into a shell double-quoted string without validation or escaping.
ollama list parsing splits on whitespace and joins columns 2:4, which is brittle if column layout changes.
The module docstring claims stdlib-only but imports project-local registry and systemd modules and shells out to du and ollama.
standouts: Dual-path swap covering both declarative config.model and imperative serve-script regex rewrite.
In-place mutation of a shell script's default argument as the legacy swap mechanism.
Human-readable inventory that aggregates two unrelated model stores (HF cache and Ollama).
conventions: Module-level constants (HUB, _MODEL_RE) and docstrings for module and public functions.
Returns (ok, message) tuples for CLI-style operations.
Early guard clauses for unknown services and unsupported manage types.
Uses project-local registry and systemd abstractions rather than direct systemctl calls.
concerns: Redundant second user-unit check in the legacy branch.
No backup before rewriting the serve script; a failed write could leave the script corrupted and the service stopped.
model_id is not validated or shell-escaped before being inserted into a double-quoted string.
ollama list output parsing is position-dependent and fragile.
Service is stopped before the script is rewritten, so a write failure leaves it down.
notes: A small operations helper that bridges old script-based and new config-driven service definitions to swap models under a local user systemd unit.
questions: What is the exact schema of the registry dict and service dict used by swap()?
How does registry.serve_script(svc) resolve the path to the serve script?
What contract does systemd.control(svc, action) follow for return values and errors?
Why is swap restricted to user-unit services, and what happens for system or other manage types?
Is there any validation or allow-list for model_id elsewhere in the project?
Is the legacy script-rewrite path planned for deprecation?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a generic registry-based model swap that works for any service using a MODEL='${1:-...}' pattern; in practice it is limited to user-unit services and contains vLLM-specific messages, though it does provide both config-driven and legacy script paths.
apparent_themes: Registry abstraction over systemd user units
Backward compatibility with legacy script-based services
intention_signals: Give operators a single CLI action to inspect available local models and switch the model a running local inference service uses, while accommodating both newer config-driven service definitions and older serve-script conventions.
novelty: Combines a declarative config.model update with a regex-based in-place rewrite of a shell serve script's default argument as two coexisting swap strategies.
contribution: Provides a concrete, file-local implementation of model inventory and hot-swap for local inference services, including formatted discovery across HF-cache and Ollama plus a unified restart flow for both config-driven and legacy script-based service definitions.
summary_for_embedding: company/ops/cli/models.py implements inventory() to list local HuggingFace Hub and Ollama models and swap() to change the active model for a registry service either by updating config.model or by rewriting a shell serve script's MODEL='${1:-...}' default and restarting the systemd user unit.
intention_for_embedding: Provide operators a single CLI mechanism to inspect on-disk models and swap the model loaded by a local user-unit inference service.

symbols.does: Lists sizes and names of models in the local HuggingFace Hub cache and Ollama runtime.
symbols.description: Scans ~/.cache/huggingface/hub for models--* directories, runs du -sh to obtain each model's size, and runs ollama list to produce a formatted human-readable inventory string.

symbols.does: Changes the active model for a registry service and restarts its user unit.
symbols.description: Looks up the service in the registry; if the service has a config block it sets config.model, saves the registry, and restarts. Otherwise it resolves the service's serve script, rewrites the MODEL='${1:-...}' default via regex, and stops/starts the systemd user unit, returning an (ok, message) tuple.
